message:
Fix stale channel waiter wakeups in transport sink and stream

Introduces pop_next_queued_waiter() helper in both sink.rs and stream.rs
to filter out stale (non-queued) waker entries during the LIFO pop path.
Previously, wakers.pop() could return a waiter whose queued flag was
already false (because the waiting task was re-polled and re-registered
a fresh waiter while the old entry remained in the stack). Waking a stale
waiter is a no-op at worst but wastes a cross-thread wake and leaves the
genuinely queued waiter un-woken, potentially causing a missed notification.

The new helper pops entries from the SmallVec until it finds one with
queued == true (Acquire ordering), discarding stale entries. Both
ChannelSink::poll_send and ChannelStream::poll_next now use this helper
instead of raw pop().

Added test coverage for both paths:
- test_channel_sink_skips_stale_recv_waiter_entries: verifies that a
  stale recv waiter is pruned and the active waiter behind it gets woken
- test_channel_stream_skips_stale_send_waiter_entries: same for the
  send waiter path

Also includes a minor formatting adjustment in broadcast.rs tests
(reformatted a multi-line while condition for readability).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
